summary_text: **Requirements Packet** Objective: make `.pdf` files open in `Chrome Canary` by default on this Mac, while keeping `Stirling-PDF` installed and launchable after a non-destructive repair. In scope: macOS `LaunchServices` handler mapping for PDF types and filename extensions; verification of Finder double-click behavior; non-destructive repair of `Stirling-PDF` user state if the app is unstable; preservation of recoverable backups. Out of scope: uninstalling either app; deleting user data; changing browser or system-wide defaults unrelated to PDF; modifying app binaries unless repair requires a safe reinstall/refresh; external services, GitHub, or network actions. Constraints: no destructive deletion without explicit approval; prefer reversible changes; keep backups under `/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp`; treat current state as baseline where `open <pdf>` already routes to Canary; do not assume Finder behavior matches `open`; stop if a change would require app removal or user-data loss. Acceptance criteria: double-clicking any local `.pdf` in Finder opens `Chrome Canary`; `open some.pdf` also opens `Chrome Canary`; `Stirling-PDF` remains launchable manually; launching `Stirling-PDF` does not crash immediately after repair; all changed state is reversible from backup. Failure modes: Finder still honors stale per-file or per-app associations; `LaunchServices` cache remains stale after plist edits; `Chrome Canary` is not registered for the relevant PDF UTI or extension; `Stirling-PDF` crash is caused by the app bundle rather than local state; repair resets user config too aggressively. Stop conditions: if Finder behavior still routes to Stirling after verified `LaunchServices` reset; if repair requires deleting unrecoverable user data; if `Chrome Canary` cannot be registered cleanly; if a binary-level reinstall becomes necessary and would be destructive without approval. Best execution plan: verify the exact PDF handler mappings for `com.adobe.pdf`, `public.pdf`, and `.pdf`; flush `LaunchServices` and Finder caches; test Finder double-click on a local PDF; if it still misroutes, inspect per-file metadata and stale registration; only then repair `Stirling-PDF` by moving its local state aside and relaunching. **Strongest Falsification Checks** 1. Finder double-click on a known local PDF still opens `Stirling-PDF` after cache flush. 2. `open /path/to/file.pdf` and Finder disagree on the target app. 3. `LaunchServices` shows `Chrome Canary`, but a fresh PDF file still inherits a hidden per-file association. 4. `Stirling-PDF` crashes even after user-state reset, which would implicate the app bundle rather than local data. 5. `Chrome Canary` lacks a valid PDF handler registration despite appearing in the plist.
